publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2023-10-14 00:18:22 UTC.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: English, Latin script.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Malcolm W. Lewis, Jr. (1925-2014) was an American composer from Cuba, New York. The Malcolm W. Lewis papers predominantly contains music composed and arranged by Lewis and recordings of performances dating 1952-2012.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: The Malcolm W. Lewis papers contain music composed and arranged by Malcom W. Lewis, Jr. (1925-2014) and recordings of performances dating 1952-2012. The collection also contains correspondence, photography, music catalogs, programs and concert ephemera, music theory teaching materials, and other items relating to Lewis's music career.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Malcolm W. Lewis papers, #8830. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Malcolm W. Lewis Jr. (1925-2014) was born on November 14, 1925 in Cuba, New York. He was the son of Malcolm Sr. and Thelma (Windsor) Lewis. He graduated from Cuba High School in 1941 and went on to study at Ithaca College. His education was interrupted when he was drafted to serve during World War II in the 10th Naval Division in Puerto Rico. Honorably discharged, he returned to Ithaca College to complete his education, receiving his B.S. in Music Education and a B.M. and M.M. in Music Composition. He also studied composition with Robert Palmer at Cornell University. He continued his studies at the Juilliard School, New York University, and Eastern Montana College. Lewis lived and taught in Montana for nine years, where he was president of the Billings Art Institute and the music representative for the Montana Institute of the Arts. He also maintained a private piano studio. Upon returning to Ithaca in 1962, he began teaching at Ithaca College. He was especially proud of the two courses he designed and taught: "The Analysis of 20th-Century Music" and "Music Composition for Non-Majors." From 1965 to 1969, he was the tour leader for the Ithaca College Study Abroad Summer Music Tours in Europe. He retired from Ithaca College in 1988 after teaching for 26 years.

Lewis was a commissioned composer for the Susquehanna Symphony Orchestra and The Society for New Music in Syracuse, New York. He received Meet the Composer and National Endowment for the Arts Awards. His compositions for piano, orchestra, percussion ensemble, band, vocal and choral music, and chamber ensemble have been published by Dorn, Ensemble Music, Ansburg Press, and TAP (Trombone Association Publishing of Western Massachusetts), and have been recorded on Redwood, Golden Crest, and MMC records. His compositions have been performed throughout the United States, Canada, and Europe. Alongside music, Lewis was also an accomplished artist who studied sculpture at the Clay Club (now SculptureCenter) in New York City, painting at Cornell, art at the Eastern Montana College of Education, and taught art at the Bozeman Central School in 1961. While on sabbatical in London in 1975, he studied at the Camden Art Center.

controlaccess.subject: Composers.
Composers -- 20th century.
Composers -- United States.
controlaccess.persname: Lewis, Malcolm, 1925-2014 -- Bagatelles, harpsichord, clarinet, trumpet, viola.
Lewis, Malcolm, 1925-2014 -- Elegy for a hollow man.

did.unittitle: American Society of University Composers, Proceedings of the Second Annual Conferece

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Biographical sketches of Lewis. Includes an autobiographical sketch and a piece written by a student.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Personal and professional correspondence. Includes correspondence and other materials from the New York Federation of Music Clubs 1993 composition competition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Miscellaneous drawings … of scroll decorations for headers for compositions.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Family trees and genealogy research on the Lewis and Gibson (Lewis's wife, Carol's) families.

did.unittitle: Hans Schmidt, "Real Estate of the Dead"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Short story.

did.unittitle: "An Introduction and Analysis of Allegro Barbaro by Bartok," paper

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A paper written by Lewis in partial fulfillment of the requirement for a degree of Bachelor of Music in Music Theory.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Programs, correspondence, and student recital programs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Music catalogs compiled by Lewis and his family, includes a comprehensive catalog of Lewis's compositions ("Mac's Music Catalog") arranged by instrument family. Also includes track listings for various CDs that features Lewis's compositions.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs of Lewis and sculptures he created at The Clay Club/SculptureCenter in New York City. Includes correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Four paintings with the initials H.S.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Various poems and short speeches written by Lewis and others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Posters from the Susquehanna Symphony Orchestra, Cornell University, and the SUNY Cortland College-Community orchestra. Includes a copy of a 1981 issue of the Ithaca College News highlighting the publication of three compositions by Lewis.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Various event and recital programs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Publishing agreements, royalty statements, copyright statements, and sales catalogs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials related to Robert Palmer, Professor of Composition at Cornell, and his 7th birthday celebration in 1990. Includes materials related to Wendell G. Earle, Professor of Marketing at Cornell.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Newsletters, correspondence, season programs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Season programs, correspondence from Sheldon Blair (conductor).

did.unittitle: 3 Inventions for alto and tenor saxophone
3 Inventions for alto and tenor saxophone [oversize]
Angels We Have Heard on High and Deck the Halls for clarinets and percussion
Bartok Miniatures for Clarinet and Flute
Benedictus 1505 for alto and baritone saxophone

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: See Brass subseries for Benedictus 1505 transcribed for trumpet and trombone.

did.unittitle: Chanson 1554 Arcadelt, for two alto and baritone saxophones

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "In Dich Habich Gehoffet" by Bach, "Herr Gott, Nun Sei Gepresiset" by Bach, "Gelobet Seist Du, Jesu Christ" by Sameul Scheidt, and "Hear Christ, Der Einig Gottes Sohn" by Johann G. Walter.

did.unittitle: Concerto for Clarinet and Wind Ensemble

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Binder-bound copy. Commissioned by the Cornell University Wind Ensemble and director Marice Stith for Gerald Zampino. Depicts memories of the Holocaust.

did.unittitle: Concerto for Horn and Wind Ensemble
Dance of the Pixies, clarinet and flute

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Passapied, Sarabanda, Bouree, Minuet, Gigue. Published by Double Dolphin Press.

did.unittitle: Duo Sonatina for woodwind and string duets

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Duets for two clarinets/clarinet and bassoon (1995), clarinet and cello (1995), and viola and cello (1996). Dedicated to Barbara Rabin, George Macero, and the Society for New Music, Syracuse, New York.

did.unittitle: Electric Flash Gallop for flute choir
Fanfare for flute and bass flute
For John Oberbrummer and his flute choir
For Solo Flute for Georgetta Maiolo
Forlhney Duets in Cannon for oboes
In Memoriam for Hilda Tanner for solo flute and piano

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes sheet music for violin.

did.unittitle: Lament and Caprice for alto and baritone saxophone
Lament and Caprice for alto and baritone saxophone [oversize]
Little March for three recorders, Handel
Organon I Robert Palmer, for two clarinets
Parade of the Tin Soldiers for flute

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: For elementary instruction.

did.unittitle: Regrets to J.C. for horn, clarinet, bass clarinet, and bassoon

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Allein zu dur, Herr Jesu Christ" by Pachelbel, "Nunfreuteuch, Lieben Christen" by Bach, and "Ach Gott Und Herr" by Walter, arranged by Lewis. Incldues a fourth untitled saxophone quartet.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Song," "Madrigal," and "Spring Song" for Soprano, Alto, and Tenor Saxophone. Published by Dorn Publishing in 1981.

did.unittitle: Sonata (A Posy for Papa Haydn) for recorder trios
Suite for NYSSMA All State Flute Choir

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: For 7 flutes, alto flute, and bass flute.

did.unittitle: Suite for NYSSMA All State Flute Choir [oversize]
Tesserae, Tesseri, and Mosiacs for piccolo and flutes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Tesseri (Piccolo, 5 flutes), Tesseri Quartet (4 flutes), Tesserae I (Piccolo, 5 flutes), Tesserae (Piccolo, alto flute, bass flute), and Mosaics (Piccolo, 2 flutes, alto flute, and bass flute).

did.unittitle: Toccata Sextet for flute, oboe, clarinet, bassoon, violin, and harp
Trio Sonata for flute, clarinet, bassoon, and piano

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes variation for saxophone quartet. Also titled Quartet for Flute, Clarinet, Bassoon, and Piano.

did.unittitle: Up on the Roof Top for three alto saxophones

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: For elementary instruction.

did.unittitle: Wedding Music for Lhney and Joshua for flute, cello/violoncello, and harp

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Anadolia, Wedding March, and Romance.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Woodwind 4 for woodwind quintet

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes alternate arrangement by Clark Tyler

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Multiple copies of the same piece, titled as variations of Allegro but all including the same solo piece for bass trombone. Includes on copy of Allegro for Horn.

did.unittitle: Benedictus 1505, Antoine Brumel for trumpet and trombone

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: See Woodwinds subseries for Benedictus 1505 transcribed for saxophones.

unittitle.title: Berceuse for two bass clef instruments, Kirnberger

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: For 3 trumpets, 3 F horns, 2 trombones, and one bass trombone.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two pieces transcribed by Lewis; Bicinium, Kaspar Othmayr (1992) and Bicinium "De Passione," Erhard Bodenschatz (1990).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes score for Trombone Miniatures for trombone quartet.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two pieces transcribed by Lewis; Canon 1571, Gerard Turnhout (1992) and Canon, Lassus/Lasso (1990).

did.unittitle: Cantiones Duarum Vocum for trumpet and trombone

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pieces by Orlando di Lasso arranged by Lewis; "Cantiones Duarum Vocum," "Beatus Homo," "Que Sequitur Me," "Oculus Non Vidit," "Justus Cor Suum Tradet," "Exspectation Justorum," "Justi Tulerunt," "Scanti Mei," "Qui Vult Post Me," "Serve Bone," "Fulgebunt Justi," and "Sicut Rosa." Inlcudes a variation of "Sicut Rosa."

did.unittitle: Chanson 1554 for trumpet, French horn, and trombone

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: See Woodwind subseries for Chanson 1554 transcribed for saxophones.

did.unittitle: Concerto for Horn and Piano (4 hands)
Concerto for Horn, Strings, and Percussion

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Alternate titles: Horn Concerto, Allegro for Horn, Strings, and Percussion. Folder 25 contains individual parts for 2 violins, viola, cello, and double bass.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Alternate titles: Horn Concerto, Allegro for Horn, Strings, and Percussion

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Arranged by Lewis for Jack Covery and the Ithaca College Horn Choir. Includes "In Canon Form," a variation of "Little Song in Canon Form." Other pieces include Roundelay, The Poor Orphan, Hunting Song, ***, First Loss, and Fugue.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Arranged by Lewis for the Ithaca College Marimba Quartet with permission from Boosey & Hawks. New Hungarian Folk Song, Round Dance, From the Diary of a Fly, Major Seconds Broken & Together, Perpetuum Mobile, Ostinato.

Five Cinqains, Adelaide Crapsey, for string quartet and soprano voice

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "November Night," 'Susanna and the Elders," "Triad," "Niagara (Seen on a November Night)," and "The Warning."

Parlor Games for violin, cello, and clarinet [oversize]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Written for Ann Rylands.

scopecontent.p: Two undated string quartets.

did.unittitle: String Quartet for the Tremont Quartet

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Written originally as the solo violin piece Sardonyx

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Referred to in Lewis's music catalog as "Suite for String Quartet" but includes parts for 2 violins, viola, cello, and string bass.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: String Quartet in A Minor (2 violins, cello, and piano), String Quartet (2010) and an undated piece for String Quartet.

did.unittitle: Trio for Violin, Cello, and Piano
Two Images for Harp and Piano

Two Images for Harp, Piano, and String Quartet [oversize]

Fibonacciana for Ubaldo Valli for full orchestra

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes alternate version titled Funzial.

did.unittitle: Five Preludes for Orchestra, Opus 74, Scriabin
From Puritan Days In Nomine Domini orchestral score

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: An earlier draft of Separate Ways.

did.unittitle: Scampers scored for Ed Hacker for full orchestra and piano

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also titled "Devil Dance." I Meditation, II Temptation, and III Devil Dance (also called Elation). Includes piece, "Meditation," written in 1944.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: I David, II Pieta, III. Battle of the Centaurs and the Lapithae

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Children's piano music by Malcolm Mewis, illustrated by Donna Forbes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Six Christmas carols arranged by Lewis, "It Came Upon a Midnight Clear," "While Shepards Watched Their Flocks," "O Come All Ye Faithful (Wades Cantus Diverse," "Away In A Manger," "O Little Town of Bethlehem," and "Joy To The World."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Waltz, Schottische, Tarantella, Allemande, Polka, and Finale. Includes Twelve Tone Piano Suite.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Four chorale preludes in memory of friends and colleagues of Ithaca College; "Trostet, Trostet Meinen Lieben" for William C. Fenton, "Alles Menschen Mussen Sterben" for E. Leslie Bennett, "Du, O Schones Weltgebaude," for Robert C. Schmidt, and "Vur Deinen Thron Tret Ich Heimet" for Frank Eldridge.

did.unittitle: Four Diverse Pieces for Piano, Organ

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Commissioned by Barbara Taylor of her husband, Jack Taylor's 60th birthday. "Taylor Made", "An Unhurried Journey", "Church Windows", and "Jack in the Pulpit". Includes orchestration/conductor's score, violin and piano duets, and violin solo parts.

did.unittitle: Fugue and Fantasia on B_A_C_H for organ, orchestra, and strings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Full scores for solo organ and pedals, organ and orchestra and, organ and strings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: I Sunday School and III Small Talk were performed for the Tompkins County Junior Music Club "Music Composters of Ithaca" event, February 1977. Includes program from the event.

There Is A Fountain for organ and pedals

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Various piano solos written with animal motifs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Chant for vocal or flute, strings, and piano.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Arranged by Lewis for Less Bennett, Voice Professor at Ithaca College.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A new setting of the original tunes and additional selections by Lewis for John Gay's opera for performance at Ithaca College. Includes photocopies of the original songs.

did.unittitle: Care Selve, Handel, for voice and string quartet

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Arranged by Lewis for Less Bennett, Voice Professor at Ithaca College.

did.unittitle: Care Selve, Handel, for voice and string quartet [oversize]
A Caution for voice and piano
The Cow and Dinky for voice

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Cow arranged for treble voice and solo flute or violin and piano, and Dinky arranged for voice and piano.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Drafts and fragments of a vocial and mixed ensemble piece composed by Lewis using text from Troilus and Cressida.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "All Hail," "Holiday," "Mother Blue Goose," "The Vicious Moray Eel"

did.unittitle: Four Love Songs for Women's Voices, Alicia S. Carpenter

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Text by Alicia S. Carpenter arranged by Lewis for a capella for two soprano and alto voice; "First Love," "Deep are Loving Eyes," "True Love," and "Which Are You?"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Arranged by Lewis for Roland Bently, Ithaca College. "That's What's The Matter," "Ah May The Red Rose Live Always," "Beautiful Dreamer," and "If You've Only Got a Moustache."

did.unittitle: Halleluiah Amen for voice and piano

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes instrumentation for woodwinds and a single page for the brass score.

did.unittitle: The Hill for soprano and alto voice

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Text by Betsy Warland Van Horn. For James Porterfield and the Ithaca College Women's Choir.

did.unittitle: Hodie Christus Natus Est for choir, trombone choir, and piano

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Commissioned by the Iota Chapter of Kappa Gamma Psi, Ithaca College.

did.unittitle: The Judgement one-act opera for choir

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: For choir, 6 soloists, and keyboard.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Text by Fred Cogswell.

did.unittitle: My Heart Leaps Up for voice, clarinet, horn in F, and violin

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes an untitled three movement Bb clarinet, F horn, and violin trio that was originally collected alongside.

did.unittitle: No Name, No Date vocal piece
Old Scent of the Plum Tree for flute and voice
One, Two, Three for soprano voice

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Text by Samuel L. Albert. Soprano voice, flute, clarinet, F horn, and harpsichord

did.unittitle: Psalm 121, "I will lift mine eyes unto the hills"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: For Emily Umbarger Andrews and the Highland Presbetarian Church, Bel Air, Maryland.

did.unittitle: Psalm 16:11, "Thou Wilt Show Me the Path of Life"
Rising Night After Night for orchestra and chorus

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Text of chorus by Abba Kovner, arranged by Lewis for an international musical competition, "Holocaust and Rebirth," held by the Israeli National Council of Culture and Art in 1977-1978. Includes correspondence and materials from the competition. Includes another piece, "Father and Son," arranged using another poem by Kovner, "Night March."

did.unittitle: Rising Night After Night orchestral score [oversize]
The Rose and A Symbol for solo voice and piano

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Text by Nina Ross.

did.unittitle: The Roawn Tree for solo voice and piano

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also includes "The Gloomy Night is Gath'ring Fast."

did.unittitle: The Rowan Tree for solo vioce and piano oversize
Seascapes (Haiku No. 9) for harp and soprano voice

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Text by LaDonna Fehlberg.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: For treble clef voice, alto recorder, tenor recorder, percussion, and harpsichord or piano. "Mon Coeur Recommende A Vous," Orlando di Lassus, "Belle Qui Tiens Ma Vie Pavanne," composer unknown, "Dedans Une Plaine 'Brunette'," composer unknown, "La Romanesca Air De Dance," composer unknown, "Anaeryllis Chanson," Louis XIII, "L'amour Au Mois De Mai Chanson," J. LeFevre and "Dans Notre Village 'Brunette'," composer unknown.

did.unittitle: Sing Your Way Home for voice and piano

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Arranged by Lewis for Barbara Irish, "A Virgin Unspoiled," "Coventry Carol," "This Endris Night," 'The Seven Joys of Mary," "Blessed be That Maid Marie," and "Master's in This Hall."

did.unittitle: Strange Night for vocal, percussion, woodwinds, and piano

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Previously named "Interactions," text by Nigel Robertson.

did.unittitle: Two Epigrams, Love and The Poet
Two Epigrams, Love and The Poet / Beside the Bed [oversize]
Two Songs by Edna St. Vincent Millay for voice, flute, and harp

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Road to Arville and Two Figs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: For soprano voice, flute, clarinet, horn in F, and harpsichord.

did.unittitle: Where Does Love Go for solo voice and trumpet in C
Where Love Dwells for choir and piano
Yesterday's Child, LaDonna Fehlberg, for voice and piano
Yesterday's Child, LaDonna Fehlberg, for voice and piano [oversize]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Earlier version of the finished piece, titled "Yesterday's Three Year Old." Includes other short vocal pieces, "Tomorrow," "Sophistication," and "Never Look Back."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Full suite for 2 flutes, 3 oboes, 3 Bb clarinets, 2 bassoons, 2 horns in F, 2 Bb trumpets, 2 trombones, percussion, and strings.

did.unittitle: Chesapeake Suite full score, instrument parts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A later addition to Chesapeake Suite.

did.unittitle: Subseries IIj. Fragments, notes, and unidentified pieces
Scraps, notes, and fragments of unidentified works

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a second movement of a trumpet and piano piece, fragments of a conductor's score removed from Poem.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: In a folder originally titled "Trombone quartet/bass quartet" but does not match a named piece in catalog of works.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: For Bb trumpet, horn in F, trombone, tuba, and piano. Score in concert pitch.

did.unittitle: Unidentified score for four bass instruments

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes parts for four bass instruments inside a 6 page score for a grand staff instrument.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Score for either a piano duet or a four-hands duo.

did.unittitle: Unidentified score for viola and cello [oversize]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Folder includes a single page of a piano score not by Lewis.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Score for brass quartet, score for Bb tenor saxophone, and score for a duet using a treble clef and a grand staff.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also includes music learning and instructional books.

did.unittitle: Agay, Denes. (1956). Easy Classics to Moderns, Music for Millions Series Volume 17. New York: Consolidated Music Publishers, Inc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Various works by Alexandre Tcherepnin and an excerpt from an article written about Tcherepnin by Sylvia Rabinof.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Various works by Bela Bartok. Includes a heavy annotated version of Out of Doors for piano.

did.unittitle: Clark, Frances and Louise Goss. (1973). The Music Tree: An Plan for Musical Growth. Illinois: Summy-Birchard Company.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Time to Begin," Part B, and Part C.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two pieces by Finko. Copy of Concerto for Viola and Orchestra inscribed to Lewis from Finko, 1984.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Various pieces by Edward MacDowell. Includes The Villain.

did.unittitle: Gay, John. (1937). The Beggar's Opera. New York: The Heritage Press.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Illustrated edition of John Gay's The Beggar's Opera, with illustrations by Mariette Lydis and an introduction by A.P. Herbert.

did.unittitle: John Davison, Sonata for Euphonium, Tuba, and Piano
Ralph T. Dudgeon and the Keyed Bugle

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pieces for keyed bugle arranged by Ralph T. Dudgeon and an exceprt from his book The Keyed Bugle (1993).

did.unittitle: Walton, William. (1955). Belshazzar's Feast for Mixed Choir, Bartione Solo and Orchestra. London: Oxford University Press.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes works signed by Carol J. Gibson, Lewis' wife.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lecture materials, course notes, and manuscripts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two manuscripts.

did.unittitle: "Introductory Techniques for Writing 20th Century Music" chapter drafts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Does not include chapter 3, "Two Voice Writing"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Statement of Permission forms signed by Lewis's former students for use of their exercises or small compositions completed in his class "Introduction to Composition" for use in a project on the pedagogy of basic composition in 20th Century idioms.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes recordings of performances, class exercises, and recitals.

did.unittitle: [minicasette] Movement for Brass Quintet and Piano, Ithaca brass Quintet
[minicasette] "Dramatic Music," Society for New Music original master
